perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means all information whether of a technical, business, financial or other nature (including, without limitation, trade secrets, know-how and information relating to the technology, customers, business plan, copyrights, trademarks, patents, promotional and marketing activities, finances and other business affairs) that is or may be disclosed or imparted by one Party to the other. Confidential Information also includes any other document provided by a Party that is clearly marked or otherwise identified as “Confidential”. Confidential Information also includes both the existence and content of discussions between the Parties with respect to a potential business transaction or relationship. Confidential Information may be in any written format, including an email transmission via electronic media and oral information.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means all information whether of a technical, business, financial or other nature (including, without limitation, trade secrets, know-how and information relating to the technology, customers, business plan, copyrights, trademarks, patents, promotional and marketing activities, finances and other business affairs) that is or may be disclosed or imparted by one Party to the other. Confidential Information also includes any other document provided by a Party that is clearly marked or otherwise identified as “Confidential”, However, information that is shared orally without any documented format shall not be considered Confidential Information.. Confidential Information also includes both the existence and content of discussions between the Parties with respect to a potential business transaction or relationship. Confidential Information may be in any written format, including an email transmission via electronic media and oral information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by excluding orally shared information, which contradicts the original definition which specifies that Confidential Information 'may be in any written format, including an email transmission via electronic media and oral information.' This weakens the protection of oral disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. As used in this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means all information whether of a technical, business, financial or other nature (including, without limitation, trade secrets, know-how and information relating to the technology, customers, business plan, copyrights, trademarks, patents, promotional and marketing activities, finances and other business affairs) that is or may be disclosed or imparted by one Party to the other. Confidential Information also includes any other document provided by a Party that is clearly marked or otherwise identified as “Confidential”, However, information that is shared orally without any documented format shall not be considered Confidential Information.. Confidential Information also includes both the existence and content of discussions between the Parties with respect to a potential business transaction or relationship. Confidential Information may be in any written format, including an email transmission via electronic media and oral information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the phrase 'However, information that is shared orally without any documented format shall not be considered Confidential Information.' This directly contradicts the statement in the original and contradicted text that 'Confidential Information may be in any written format, including an email transmission via electronic media and oral information.' The change creates a clear and direct conflict regarding whether orally shared information qualifies as confidential.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that it will use diligent efforts to protect the secrecy and confidentiality of and avoid disclosure of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, including implementing equivalent security measures and degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own proprietary or confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that it will use commercially reasonable efforts to protect the secrecy and confidentiality of and avoid disclosure of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, without the need to implement any additional security measures.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'diligent efforts' to 'commercially reasonable efforts' and removing the requirement for 'implementing equivalent security measures' creates a contradiction. The new wording makes the protection standard less stringent, which contradicts the general expectation of strong confidentiality measures in a non-disclosure agreement, leading to uncertainty about the actual protective measures required. Furthermore the explicit statement of not needing to implement any additional security measures is creating an internal contradiction with the rest of the text, where security is implied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Protection of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party agrees that it will use commercially reasonable efforts to protect the secrecy and confidentiality of and avoid disclosure of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, without the need to implement any additional security measures.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by weakening the confidentiality obligations. Specifically, it changes 'diligent efforts' to 'commercially reasonable efforts' and explicitly removes the requirement to implement equivalent security measures. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, however, the change from the original text creates an in-text contradiction, because the modified clause reduces the security expectations compared to standard NDA practice. Therefore, the change introducing a lower standard of care is a clear and direct conflict, creating an inconsistency within the document about the required level of protection, leading to uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall expressly restrict the possession, knowledge, development and use of Confidential Information to its partners, employees, consultants, professional advisors, agents, subcontractors and entities controlled by the Receiving Party or hired or engaged by the Receiving Party who have: (i) been determined to have a need to know, (ii) been advised of the proprietary nature of the Confidential Information being disclosed, (iii) been advised of their obligations as set forth in this Agreement to keep such Confidential Information confidential, and (iv) been placed under an obligation to the Receiving Party to preserve Confidential Information in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall share the possession, knowledge, development and use of Confidential Information freely with its partners, employees, consultants, professional advisors, agents, subcontractors and entities controlled by the Receiving Party or hired or engaged by the Receiving Party regardless if they have: (i) been determined to have a need to know, (ii) been advised of the proprietary nature of the Confidential Information being disclosed, (iii) been advised of their obligations as set forth in this Agreement to keep such Confidential Information confidential, and (iv) been placed under an obligation to the Receiving Party to preserve Confidential Information in confidence.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'restrict' to 'share freely' directly undermines the purpose of controlling access to confidential information and directly contradicts the bullet points which outline necessary conditions for sharing the information, rendering them irrelevant and creates a direct contradiction with the main purpose of the agreement. This makes the entire agreement dubious because, it shares freely but the bullet points say otherwise, creating a In-Text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party Personnel. The Receiving Party shall share the possession, knowledge, development and use of Confidential Information freely with its partners, employees, consultants, professional advisors, agents, subcontractors and entities controlled by the Receiving Party or hired or engaged by the Receiving Party regardless if they have: (i) been determined to have a need to know, (ii) been advised of the proprietary nature of the Confidential Information being disclosed, (iii) been advised of their obligations as set forth in this Agreement to keep such Confidential Information confidential, and (iv) been placed under an obligation to the Receiving Party to preserve Confidential Information in confidence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'expressly restrict' with 'share freely,' directly contradicting the original intent of controlling access to confidential information. This creates a conflict with the bullet points (i-iv) outlining necessary conditions for sharing, effectively rendering them meaningless. The 'Contradicted Text' reiterates this 'share freely' clause. This is a direct in-text contradiction since the contract now states the Receiving Party both restricts access and shares freely, leading to ambiguity and unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will have no rights, by license or otherwise, to use or disseminate the Confidential Information except as expressly provided in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will have the right, by license or otherwise, to use or disseminate the Confidential Information even if not expressly provided in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing “no rights” to “the right” directly contradicts the terms of the agreement that aim to control how the Confidential Information must be handled and used. In this way, the 'rights' change renders the entire confidentiality agreement unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party will have the right, by license or otherwise, to use or disseminate the Confidential Information even if not expressly provided in this Agreement. No patent, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right is licensed, granted or otherwise conveyed by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Receiving Party has "no rights" to use or disseminate Confidential Information except as expressly provided. The changed text grants the Receiving Party "the right" to use or disseminate Confidential Information even if not expressly provided. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the rights of the Receiving Party concerning the Confidential Information, as highlighted in the perturbation explanation. The contradicted text re-iterates the change, further reinforcing the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) days of receiving notice from the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and verify in writing its destruction) all material embodying Confidential Information (in any form and including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information and all electronic media or records containing or derived from Confidential Information).
perturbation.changed_text: Within thirty (30) days of receiving notice from the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party may, at its discretion, return or destroy (and verify in writing its destruction) all material embodying Confidential Information (in any form and including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information and all electronic media or records containing or derived from Confidential Information).
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the original text to introduce ambiguities by changing “ten (10) days” to “thirty (30) days” and, “shall promptly return” to “may, at its discretion, return”. The original text defines the return or destruction of confidential materials as a requirement, with the change the text makes this return optional. This creates an in-text contradiction since the Receiving Party may be required to comply with the agreement, and not complying with the request, may be seen as a breach of contract. The text is very unclear and introduces ambiguities on when and how it should be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information. Within thirty (30) days of receiving notice from the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party may, at its discretion, return or destroy (and verify in writing its destruction) all material embodying Confidential Information (in any form and including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information and all electronic media or records containing or derived from Confidential Information).
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the original text by changing "ten (10) days" to "thirty (30) days” and, “shall promptly return” to “may, at its discretion, return”. This makes the return/destruction of confidential materials optional. The contradicted text states the same clause, implying compliance is optional, causing ambiguity. The original text defines the return or destruction of confidential materials as a requirement, with the change the text makes this return optional. This contradiction makes the terms of the agreement unclear and creates an in-text contradiction, since it is unclear whether the Receiving Party has to return/destroy confidential information or not. This meets the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition, because key obligations are defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult.
